scope.summary: Implement UV-space texture bake that renders the 3D draped garment surface (albedo, normal, AO) into UV atlas texture space via an offscreen wgpu UV-space render pass and stores baked maps as artifact refs
acceptance_criteria: A tailor_bake_textures Tauri command accepts garment_id, simulation_run_id, and a BakeOptions struct (resolution: u32, channels: Vec<BakeChannel>) and returns artifact refs for each baked map
BakeChannel enum covers Albedo, Normal, AmbientOcclusion, and Displacement
The bake renders from the 3D draped mesh into UV atlas space using an offscreen wgpu render pass (not a CPU raycast)
Baked map artifact refs are stored in tailor_pbr_materials columns (normal_map_ref, roughness_map_ref) when the operator assigns baked maps to the garment
The tailor_bake_textures command emits a TailorPbrMapsGenerated EventLedger event with gen_mode = 'bake_uv_space' in the payload
The command is async and MUST NOT block the Tailor panel UI
proof_targets: HBR-VIS: baked normal map loaded into TailorPbrMaterial shows surface wrinkle detail from a draped silk panel in the solid viewport render
scope.risk_if_missed: Without UV-space baking, Tailor cannot produce high-fidelity game-ready textures capturing simulation wrinkle detail. Every exported garment requires additional manual texture work in Blender or Substance Painter, negating the production pipeline efficiency.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
